_meta.keyKnowledge: We reflect on, analyse, evaluate and document our own and others' drama to inform our choices as both artists and audience members.

_source.w: We reflect on, analyse, evaluate and document our own and others' drama to inform our choices as both artists and audience members.
y: Critical evaluation at this level requires us to engage with drama as both practitioners and informed audience members.
Rigorous reflection and analysis are fundamental to mature artistic practice.
Documenting our evaluative thinking demonstrates the depth of our engagement with the art form.
eg: We write detailed critical responses to professional theatre productions.
We evaluate our own and others' drama work against artistic intentions and audience reception.
We analyse how directors and playwrights use theatrical conventions to construct meaning.
We maintain comprehensive documentation of our creative process and critical reflections.
We use theatrical terminology with precision when analysing and discussing drama.
We engage in structured peer critique that is both rigorous and constructive.
